data.title: Vestas to build €250M wind turbine plant in Scotland
data.description:
Vestas Wind Systems (VWDRY) said Wednesday it plans to spend more than €250M to build a nacelle and hub factory in Scotland to meet growing demand for offshore wind in the U.K. and Europe, with an eye toward starting production by 2029 or 2030.

The plant, still subject to a final investment decision conditional on securing sufficient U.K. orders from subsidy auctions, would produce parts for Vestas' (VWDRY) flagship offshore wind turbine, the V236-15.0 MW, and create as many as 500 direct jobs, the company said.

The Vestas (VWDRY) announcement is seen as a win for the U.K. government’s clean energy agenda and U.K. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband, who has faced criticism for hastening the decline of North Sea oil and gas, a major employer in the region.

data.title: Reed's outlines new product launches and supply chain efficiencies as company targets margin expansion in 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Reed's, Inc. (REED) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Neal Cohane, stepping in as Interim CEO and continuing as COO, addressed the leadership transition, stating, "As announced in our earnings press release, Cyril Wallace has stepped down as CEO. I will assume the additional role of Interim CEO while continuing as Chief Operating Officer, and I will also join Reed's Board of Directors." Cohane emphasized a focus on operational priorities, profitable growth, and stabilizing the business, noting sequential improvements in net sales, gross margin, and net loss.
  • Cohane outlined strategies to optimize the manufacturing network, implement tighter operational controls, enhance sourcing and procurement, and lower per unit costs, with ongoing efforts in packaging optimization, freight, logistics efficiencies, and SKU rationalization.
  • The company is targeting expansion into under-penetrated channels such as food service and convenience, describing them as "meaningful white space opportunities for the Reed's brand."
  • New product launches include Reed's Ginger Ale Cranberry and Blackberry in Q2 2026 and a line of nonalcoholic mixers in early Q3 2026. Cohane reported, "The core item, the Reed's Ginger Ale, remains the #1 premium ginger ale in total U.S. and continues to grow and is plus 13.7% in dollar sales over the past 52 weeks."
  • Digital and cultural marketing efforts include new campaigns on Instacart, walmart.com, albertsons.com, and a social media strategy, with partnerships involving influencers such as Hayden Hurst.
  • Reed's is expanding internationally, with a presence at the sugar and wine trade show in Chengdu, China, and launching Reed's U Oxygen energy drink targeting health-conscious consumers.
  • Cohane highlighted a $10 million underwritten public offering and uplisting to the NYSE American, stating, "This transaction strengthens our balance sheet and enhances our financial flexibility providing additional capital to support key growth initiatives."
  • Douglas McCurdy, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "Net sales for the fourth quarter of '25 were $7.5 million compared to $9.7 million in the year ago quarter... Gross margin was 20% compared to 30% in the year ago quarter... Delivery and handling costs were reduced by 35% to $1.1 million during the fourth quarter of 2025."

Outlook

  • Cohane stated, "Looking ahead in 2026, we are focused on expanding our presence in under-penetrated channels, particularly food service and convenience."
  • The company expects margin expansion through "more disciplined trade spend, improved pricing and promotional effectiveness and continued operational efficiency gains across our supply chain and organization."
  • International expansion in Asia remains a priority, with Cohane noting a "significant long-term opportunity to extend the reach of Reed's brand and capture incremental growth."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q4 2025 were $7.5 million.
  • Gross profit for the quarter was $1.5 million, with a 20% gross margin.
  • Delivery and handling costs declined to $1.1 million, representing 14% of net sales or $2.46 per case.
  • Selling, general and administrative expenses were $4.0 million.
  • Net loss for Q4 2025 was $3.8 million or (-$0.44) per share, while EBITDA was negative $3.6 million.
  • Cash used in operating activities was $3.8 million; ending cash was $10.4 million with $9.3 million in total debt as of December 31, 2025.

Q&A

  • John (Alliance Global Partners): Asked about the cadence of distribution gains in 2026 and spring reset opportunities. Cohane replied, "We're working on it as we speak. We have the sales team aligned... we're also working on velocities, to improve velocities at store level. So we're going to be completely focused in 2026 on the customer and on our distributors."
  • John: Inquired about the path to profitability and margin initiatives flowing through the P&L. Cohane answered, "We're looking to reduce expenses, which we are doing year-over-year, quarter-over-quarter... it's going to be a combination... of reducing expenses and driving volume at store level."
  • John: Asked for details on the SodaSmarter launch and lessons learned. Cohane responded, "We're launching our new mixer line... and then we're coming back to the SodaSmarter, and we're going to be looking at improving flavors, improving formulas, and then we're going to improve execution on that at the same time."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on operational execution, distribution gains, and profitability, with a neutral tone seeking clarity on execution timelines and margin improvements.
  • Management maintained a constructive and slightly optimistic tone, emphasizing operational progress and the impact of cost-reduction efforts. Cohane's remarks on future growth and margin expansion reflected confidence without overstatement.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained pragmatic, while management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to a more action-oriented, solution-driven stance as evidenced by the focus on sequential improvements and new initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Leadership transition from Cyril Wallace to Neal Cohane as Interim CEO, with the Board initiating a search for a permanent CEO.
  • Increased emphasis on margin expansion, operational efficiencies, and expanding into new channels in the current quarter, compared to prior focus on organizational restructuring and foundational growth.
  • The company completed a $10 million public offering and uplisted to NYSE American in Q4, whereas in Q3, preparations for uplisting and balance sheet improvements were noted.
  • Delivery and handling costs continued to decline, and cost control initiatives remained central, with expanded details on SKU rationalization and procurement strategies in Q4.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from concerns about lost distribution and marketing investment in Q3 to queries about execution of distribution gains and product launches in Q4.
  • Management sentiment moved from highlighting groundwork and recovery in Q3 to demonstrating tangible progress and confidence in executing growth strategies in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the need for improved execution, particularly in distribution gains and in-store velocities.
  • The company is still in the early phases of operational improvements, acknowledging "these results reflect meaningful progress in improving execution."
  • Product launches and flavor/formulation improvements carry execution risk, with management noting ongoing work to enhance new and existing product lines.
  • Inventory management and cost controls remain areas of focus, including addressing slower-moving and obsolete inventory.
  • International expansion and new product launches involve exposure to new markets and consumer preferences.

Final Takeaway

Reed's leadership transition and enhanced financial flexibility are paired with a multipronged growth strategy centered on operational improvements, distribution expansion, and new product launches. Management highlighted sequential improvements in key financial metrics, a strengthened balance sheet from the recent public offering, and concrete plans to expand both domestically and in Asia. The company expects margin expansion through cost discipline, supply chain efficiencies, and more targeted marketing, while continuing to address risks tied to execution and profitability. Investors are encouraged by the early signs of progress and the company's focus on sustainable growth and value creation going forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Vertiv rallies to record high as HSBC sees strong upside from AI demand
data.description:
Shares of Vertiv Holdings (VRT) climbed to a record high on Wednesday as investor enthusiasm around artificial intelligence infrastructure continued to build, supported by a bullish outlook from HSBC analysts.

The firm initiated coverage of Vertiv (VRT) with a Buy rating and a price target of $325, implying roughly 27% upside from recent levels. HSBC views Vertiv as a “pure play” on data center power and cooling, positioning it at the center of one of the fastest growing segments in the AI supply chain.

AI capex surge driving equipment demand

HSBC expects a sharp acceleration in capital spending by hyperscale cloud providers, forecasting a roughly 91% increase in U.S. cloud capex in 2026 driven by AI.  This surge is expected to translate directly into higher demand for infrastructure equipment, particularly in power management and cooling systems.

The analysts estimate the total addressable market for power and cooling equipment at about $156 billion in 2026, up approximately 67% year over year. This reflects both rising compute intensity and increasing complexity in data center design.

Vertiv positioned as integrated supplier

HSBC favors vertically integrated suppliers and identifies Vertiv (VRT) alongside Eaton (ETN) and Schneider Electric as best positioned to capture growth from evolving data center architectures. 

Vertiv stands out for its broad exposure across both power and thermal management systems. According to HSBC’s analysis of the supply chain, the company derives roughly 80% of its revenue from data center related activity, one of the highest concentrations among peers.

This positioning allows Vertiv (VRT) to participate across multiple components of the data center buildout, from uninterruptible power systems to cooling technologies.

Order growth, backlog signal strong demand

The report highlights strong recent momentum in Vertiv’s business. The company recorded a 252% year over year increase in fourth quarter order intake and more than 50% sequential backlog growth. 

HSBC said these trends reflect both the scale and urgency of hyperscaler procurement, as customers accelerate deployment timelines to support AI workloads.

Shift To Higher Power Density And Liquid Cooling

The next phase of data center evolution is expected to require significantly higher power density, with rack requirements rising from roughly 10 kilowatts today toward 1 megawatt for next generation chips. 

This shift is driving rapid adoption of liquid cooling technologies, which HSBC identifies as the fastest growing segment within thermal management. The firm expects cooling solutions to become an increasingly important share of overall infrastructure spending.

Vertiv has already expanded its capabilities in this area through acquisitions, including its purchase of liquid cooling specialist CoolTera, positioning the company to benefit from the transition.

Technology Shifts Create Additional Opportunity
HSBC also points to longer term changes in power architecture, including a potential transition to 800-volt direct current systems. While still in early stages, this shift could reshape the competitive landscape and favor suppliers with expertise across multiple voltage levels and system components.

In parallel, constraints in grid infrastructure and long lead times for high voltage equipment are reinforcing demand for integrated solutions, including onsite power generation and advanced distribution systems.

Valuation Reflects Growth But Upside Remains
Vertiv trades at a premium to many industrial peers, reflecting its exposure to high growth end markets. HSBC estimates the company’s valuation at about 28.6 times forward enterprise value to ebitda and roughly 40 times forward earnings for 2026.

Despite this, the firm believes the growth outlook justifies the valuation, citing strong return metrics and sustained demand visibility.

Outlook Supported By Structural Demand Trends
HSBC’s broader conclusion is that the AI infrastructure cycle remains in its early stages, with spending constrained more by execution capacity than by demand.

As data center buildouts accelerate globally and workloads expand, suppliers of critical power and cooling equipment are expected to see continued order growth. Within that group, Vertiv’s focused exposure and integrated offering position it as a key beneficiary of the trend.

data.title: Eaton positioned to benefit from AI power demand: BNP Paribas
data.description:
Eaton (ETN) is increasingly emerging as a critical beneficiary of the artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, according to BNP Paribas senior equity research analyst Andrew Buscaglia.

Following investor meetings across Europe, the firm said in a March 24 research note that it has higher conviction in Eaton (ETN) as a 2026 top pick, citing what it views as a continued underappreciation of the company’s role in enabling AI-driven power demand.

Buscaglia emphasized that power availability remains a key bottleneck in the expansion of AI data centers. Eaton’s (ETN) portfolio of electrical components and systems positions it as a central supplier in addressing that constraint, providing infrastructure necessary to support accelerating compute demand.

Data Center Exposure Provides Visibility

BNP Paribas highlighted Eaton’s (ETN) growing exposure to data centers as a key driver of forward visibility. Approximately one-third of the company’s Electrical Americas backlog, estimated at roughly $15 billion for 2025, is tied to data center demand. The firm noted that customer engagement is increasing, with improved insight into long-term buildout plans.

The transition toward 800-volt systems is also expected to be favorable for Eaton (ETN), as management believes it will increase content per installation while maintaining strong pricing dynamics.

Margin Expansion Expected As Headwinds Ease

While Eaton’s (ETN) Electrical Americas segment has faced margin pressure from capacity expansion, BNP Paribas expects those headwinds to subside. The firm forecasts segment margins exceeding 31.5% by the end of 2026, with a path to more than 32% over the longer term.

This outlook addresses investor concerns regarding margin sustainability and supports the broader thesis of improving profitability as recent investments begin to normalize.

Boyd Acquisition Enhances Strategic Positioning

The recent acquisition of Boyd, a provider of liquid cooling solutions for servers, is viewed as a meaningful strategic addition. BNP Paribas noted that Boyd holds a leading position in in-server cooling, particularly in cold plate technology, with market share above 40%.

The transaction expands Eaton’s (ETN) exposure across the power and thermal management stack, effectively positioning the company from grid infrastructure through to chip-level cooling. The firm believes this integrated offering is not yet fully reflected in investor expectations.

Earnings Estimates And Valuation Updated

BNP Paribas modestly adjusted its 2026 earnings per share estimate to $13.40 from $13.45, while increasing projections for 2027 and 2028 to $16.00 and $18.80, respectively, reflecting the accretive impact of the Boyd acquisition.

The firm raised its price target on Eaton (ETN) shares to $460 from $440, based on unchanged valuation multiples of 28 times price-to-earnings and 21.5 times enterprise value to earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization applied to 2027 estimates. The revised target implies approximately 28 percent upside from current levels. BNP Paribas also outlined a bull case valuation of approximately $555 per share.

Outlook Remains Constructive

Overall, BNP Paribas expects Eaton (ETN) to benefit from sustained demand for AI-related infrastructure, improving margin dynamics, and increased earnings contribution from recent strategic initiatives. The firm anticipates a return to consistent earnings outperformance and upward estimate revisions as backlog converts and capacity-related headwinds diminish.

Buscaglia’s central conclusion is that Eaton’s (ETN) role in the AI ecosystem remains underrecognized, with further upside likely as execution and end-market demand continue to develop.

data.title: Oil prices are in a ‘hawkish’ range for the Fed – BofA
data.description:
Oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) have entered a “hawkish” range for the Federal Reserve, potentially complicating the central bank’s policy path in the wake of the Iran shock, according to Aditya Bhave, senior U.S. economist at Bank of America.

“We think oil prices are in the ‘hawkish’ range for now,” Bhave said, noting that the Fed’s response will depend heavily on both the duration and magnitude of the current energy price disruption.

A moderate and sustained oil shock represents the specific scenario most likely to trigger a more aggressive stance from the Fed, according to the economist.

 

 

 

“There is a range of outcomes—where the shock is sustained but moderate—such that the Fed would turn hawkish because it’s more worried about inflation,” he explained.

By contrast, if the conflict is resolved quickly, oil prices could fall significantly, allowing the Fed to reinstate its dovish bias.

The economist noted that larger disruptions create different dynamics for policymakers.

“Downside risks to the labor market will increase non-linearly with the size of the shock,” Bhave said. He explained that while inflation risks initially rise with larger shocks, they eventually fall due to demand destruction, which would prompt the Fed to become more dovish to protect employment rather than combat rising prices.

Broader financial market conditions also factor into the Fed’s calculus, according to Bhave.

“Negative wealth effects from a sustained equity selloff would exacerbate downside risks to labor and limit the upside to inflation,” he observed. This means a prolonged stock market downturn would reinforce pressure on the Fed to ease rather than tighten.

While additional rate hikes are not Bank of America’s primary expectation, the senior economist concluded that current “Goldilocks” oil prices mean such a move cannot be dismissed.

“Hikes are far from our base case, but they’re a risk worth considering,” Bhave said, emphasizing that the moderate price range keeps the possibility of tighter policy on the table.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $88.8 on Friday, while Brent futures (CO1:COM) were at $100.4 per barrel.

data.title: Disney cancels $1B investment in OpenAI; D'Amaro expected to assess Epic Games cuts
data.description:
Media conglomerate Disney (DIS) is cancelling its $1B investment in OpenAI after the ChatGPT owner announced Wednesday that it will shutter its AI-powered short video generation app Sora, according to multiple media reports.

The deal, which included a licensing pact for over 200 Disney characters for the Sora app, was supposed to be a gateway for OpenAI to tap into valuable Hollywood intellectual property amid the film industry's fight against AI startups that violate copyrighted content. 

"As the nascent AI field advances rapidly, we respect OpenAI’s decision to exit the video generation business and to shift its priorities elsewhere," according to a Disney spokesperson. "We appreciate the constructive collaboration between our teams and what we learned from it, and we will continue to engage with AI platforms to find new ways to meet fans where they are while responsibly embracing new technologies that respect IP and the rights of creators."

The news also comes about a week into new Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro's tenure.

Separately, D'Amaro is expected to evaluate Disney's exposure in Epic Games, the company behind the popular Fortnite game, after it announced job cuts on Tuesday due to slowing user engagement.

Disney made a $1.5B investment back in February 2024 to take a 9% stake in Epic Games; D'Amaro spearheaded that deal under previous CEO Bob Iger.

data.title: AI presents most significant catalyst cybersecurity industry has experienced in 20 years: Wedbush
data.description:
The rise of artificial intelligence has created the most significant catalyst the cybersecurity industry has experienced in two decades, according to Wedbush.

The financial firm finds industry stalwarts such as CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Zscaler (ZS), Check Point (CHKP), and Rubrik (RBRK) stand out as the most likely winners of the AI windfall over the coming years.

"We note that today we estimate cyber security represents roughly 5% of IT budgets and will likely be doubling over the next few years to 10% as protecting the tidal wave of agents, AI use cases, and broader technology push poses massive risks to many organizations we spoke with on the floor," said Wedbush analysts following their discussions at the RSA Conference this week in San Francisco. "In essence, AI makes cyber security even a higher priority but, importantly, will attach this technology to many strategic AI deployments, which will translate into hundreds of billions of dollars incrementally coming into the industry over the coming years."

AI startups OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) have released their own cybersecurity agents recently, such as Codex Security and Claude Code Security, respectively. However, Wedbush does not believe these types of developments will diminish the need for cybersecurity vendors.

"The increased use of AI has dramatically lowered the cost, skill, and time required to execute sophisticated attacks while massively elevating their scale/precision," Wedbush added. "AI agents and autonomous workflows dramatically increase the attack surface further, with more APIs, more machine identities, more lateral movement risk, and more cloud-native workloads creating entirely new vectors that legacy point solutions were never designed to address. Critically, AI does not reduce the need for endpoint, identity, cloud, and SOC automation; it multiplies it as more enterprises deploy LLM-powered agents….cyber becomes the enforcement layer of AI, not a casualty of it."

Most cybersecurity stocks were moving higher on Wednesday. Check Point had inched up 0.8%, CrowdStrike had ticked up 1%, Palo Alto had increased 1.5%, and Zscaler had edged up 3%. SentinelOne (S) had popped up 5%.

data.title: Canada discussing potential Keystone XL revival with Trump administration officials
data.description:
Canadian officials spoke to Trump administration representatives about a proposed revival of part of the canceled Keystone XL oil pipeline at a meeting in Houston this week, Canada's Natural Resources Minister Tim Hodgson said Tuesday, according to Reuters.

The project proposed by pipeline company South Bow (SOBO) and ‌U.S. partner Bridger Pipeline was one of the ‌topics Hodgson said he and Canada's Ambassador to the U.S., Mark Wiseman, discussed with U.S. Energy Secretary Wright and U.S. Interior Secretary Burgum.

Canada is positioning the potential of a new cross-border oil pipeline as a way it ‌can help the U.S. achieve energy security, Hodgson ⁠said in an interview at the ‌CERAWeek by S&P Global conference.

The U.S. is the world's largest producer of oil at 12M-13M bbl/day, "but they consume 20M," Hodgson said, "and they understand that Canada provides ~63% of that difference."

Hodgson said he also made it clear ‌at this week's meeting that Canada is aggressively working to expand its oil exports to non-U.S. markets by completing a planned 300K bbl/day expansion of the Trans Mountain pipeline that runs from ⁠Alberta to the Pacific Coast.

The Keystone XL project, ​which was canceled by former President Biden, is fully permitted ⁠on the Canadian side, but a presidential permit would be needed for the pipeline to cross the Canada-U.S. border, and state regulatory permits also would be needed.

"The President's entire energy team has been working diligently with our partners ​in Canada to work through the permitting process," an unnamed Trump administration told Reuters.

data.title: Edap targets $50M–$54M HIFU revenue for 2026 with continued U.S. and international expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EDAP TMS S.A. (EDAP) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ryan Rhodes described 2025 as a transformative year, highlighting "39% revenue growth in our core HIFU business and record commercial performance for Focal One." Rhodes indicated that much of the growth was driven by accelerated adoption in the U.S., citing "record system placements and strong procedure growth." He emphasized that "the fourth quarter was the strongest quarter in the company's history for HIFU revenue, representing an increase of 34% over the same period last year."
  • Rhodes detailed commercial achievements, including a record 15 Focal One placements in Q4—14 of which were cash sales—with 10 in the U.S., the highest quarterly total to date. He noted expanding adoption among leading academic centers and community hospitals, including placements at Aurora St. Luke's Medical Center in Wisconsin and four new systems in Pennsylvania, as well as conversions from legacy HIFU technology at major institutions.
  • International expansion was marked by first-time sales in India and Argentina, with additional system sales in Italy and Spain, further strengthening the company's global footprint. Rhodes noted, "our Focal One capital sales momentum also continues to expand in existing regions as well as new emerging markets."
  • Regulatory progress included FDA clearance for the latest Focal One Robotic HIFU platform with advanced ultrasound imaging, supporting future AI-driven developments. Rhodes highlighted new reimbursement rates, stating "a national facility payment average of $9,671, representing a 4.6% increase versus 2025."
  • On clinical expansion, Rhodes pointed to ongoing studies in endometriosis and BPH, including a new clinical trial in South America in collaboration with Mount Sinai Health System.
  • CFO Ken Mobeck reported, "HIFU revenue for the full year 2025 was EUR 33.1 million, an increase of 39% as compared to HIFU revenue of EUR 23.8 million for the full year 2024." Mobeck explained the total revenue decrease was due to a "27% decline in our noncore distribution and ESWL businesses, which offset the 39% growth in core HIFU business."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated 2026 guidance, stating, "we expect core HIFU revenue to be in the range of USD 50 million to USD 54 million, representing 34% to 45% growth over 2025." Combined noncore revenue was guided to "USD 22 million to USD 26 million."
  • Rhodes explained, "The combination of these priorities, commercial execution, indication expansion and continued technology innovation and leadership underpins our confidence in our 2026 outlook and beyond."

Financial Results

  • HIFU revenue in Q4 2025 was EUR 11.7 million, a 34% increase from EUR 8.8 million in Q4 2024. Total revenue for the quarter was EUR 18.9 million, down 7% from EUR 20.3 million in Q4 2024, due to a 38% decline in noncore segments.
  • Gross margin for the quarter was EUR 8.1 million, compared to EUR 9.1 million in Q4 2024. Gross margin on net sales was 42.6%, down from 44.8%, impacted by tariffs and inventory reserves.
  • Operating expenses were EUR 13.2 million versus EUR 12.8 million in Q4 2024, primarily from increased HIFU investments. Operating loss was EUR 5.2 million compared to EUR 3.7 million, and net loss was EUR 8.2 million or EUR 0.22 per share, compared to EUR 1.9 million or EUR 0.05 per share last year. The increase was attributed to "a EUR 2.5 million noncash charge related to warrants and interest expense" and "a EUR 2 million negative currency impact."
  • Inventory decreased to EUR 10.9 million from EUR 13.8 million at the end of Q3. Total cash and cash equivalents rose to EUR 17.4 million from EUR 10.6 million, reflecting the EIB Tranche A drawdown.

Q&A

  • Michael Sarcone, Jefferies: asked for details on procedure versus capital sales growth and Q1 trends. Ryan Rhodes responded, "Procedure growth, again, we saw a notable increase Q4, 28% over prior year...we look outward and see a strong year for us, both in terms of capital sales as well as procedure growth." Mobeck added, "With our installed base now at 165 units, that's also going to lead to disposable sales growth and service growth as well."
  • Sarcone asked about Q1 procedure trends and storm impacts. Rhodes answered, "No impact that I can point to. I would say, generally, we see a nice ramp developing. ... Q1 is ramping as planned."
  • Joseph Downing, Piper Sandler: questioned HIFU revenue seasonality and noncore wind-down. Mobeck confirmed, "Q4 to be the highest growth quarter...the lowest quarter will be in Q3. ... ESWL is roughly 20% to 25% of the noncore."
  • Swayampakula Ramakanth, H.C. Wainwright: asked about margin impacts from tariffs and strategy. Mobeck said, "We have budgeted about $2.5 million in tariffs in 2026 to be conservative, and we're just going to continually monitor what everything is happening from the government regarding those."
  • Ramakanth also asked about cash sales and pricing strategy. Rhodes replied, "cash sales will be our lead theme going forward...We have a price increase that went in place last year. We haven't changed our pricing strategy at the beginning of this year, and we see our average selling prices tend to hold or even slightly increase."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of growth in procedures and capital sales, seasonality in revenue, the impact of tariffs, and the transition of revenue mix, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarifications on guidance and operational levers.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing commercial momentum, strategic execution, and guidance reiteration. Rhodes stated, "we are confident in our ability to deliver sustainable growth and create long-term shareholder value."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained consistent, while management demonstrated increased confidence, particularly citing record performance and strong 2026 guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reiterated and raised its focus on HIFU growth, with explicit 2026 guidance for core HIFU revenue and noncore segment management. Strategic emphasis shifted even more toward capital sales and recurring procedure revenues, whereas previously, there was more discussion of clinical validation and market access progress.
  • Key metrics saw HIFU revenue growth rates increase both year-over-year and sequentially, while noncore revenue continued to decline as planned.
  • Management's confidence heightened, with stronger language around commercial execution and market penetration compared to the previous quarter.
  • Analysts’ questions in both quarters centered on growth drivers, reimbursement, and operational risks, but recent focus increased on the sustainability of cash sales and pricing stability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged exposure to tariffs on finished goods shipped from France to the U.S., budgeting $2.5 million for 2026 and noting ongoing monitoring of government policy.
  • The decline in noncore distribution and ESWL businesses was cited as a known risk, with strategy to manage and potentially exit less accretive agreements.
  • Currency impacts and noncash charges below the operating line contributed to increased net loss, as noted by the CFO.
  • Analysts inquired about the duration and materiality of noncore revenue and the potential for further margin pressure from tariffs.

Final Takeaway

EDAP TMS S.A. closed 2025 with record HIFU revenue growth and robust commercial momentum, particularly in the U.S. and key international markets. The company reinforced its leadership in robotic focal therapy with new FDA-cleared technology and strengthened reimbursement, while reiterating 2026 core HIFU revenue guidance of $50 million to $54 million. Management outlined clear strategic priorities in commercial execution, clinical expansion, and continued innovation, supporting confidence in sustainable growth and shareholder value creation for the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Jensen Huang, Mark Zuckerberg among Trump's tech council
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has appointed some of the biggest names in business, including Meta Platforms' (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Oracle's (ORCL) Executive Chairman Larry Ellison, and Nvidia's (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, to a technology panel to advise him on science and technology.

The White House said that under Trump, the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, or PCAST, will focus on topics related to the opportunities and challenges that emerging technologies present to the American workforce and ensure all Americans thrive in the Golden Age of Innovation.

PCAST will be co-chaired by David Sacks, who has served as White House AI and crypto czar, and Michael Kratsios, another technology advisor, according to the White House's press release.

Trump appointed 13 members, which also include Advanced Micro Devices' (AMD) CEO Lisa Su and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Co-Founder Sergey Brin.

"We’re delighted to see NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang appointed to this council and to have the opportunity to advance American leadership in AI," said Nvidia in a post on X. "America’s strength in science and technology depends on a vibrant ecosystem of researchers, developers, and industry working together to drive innovation and global leadership."

The White House said that PCAST may be composed of up to 24 members. Additional members will be appointed in the near future, the White House added.

Update: The story was updated with Nvidia's post on X.
